item.title: She Was a Famous Millennial Feminist. Her Polyamory Memoir Is Heartbreaking.
item.description: Almost every ideology can be wielded to make women feel that they’re failing.

item.title: America Is Abandoning Morality
item.description: The bombing campaign Mr. Trump is threatening in Iran could affect millions of Iranian civilians’ access to water, electricity and food.

item.title: MAGA Was Supposed to Be Antiwar. Nope.
item.description: It might be time to ditch some common assumptions about Republicans.

item.title: Can Trump Claim Victory in Iran?
item.description: Is this really what winning the war with Iran looks like? Mark Dubowitz, the chief executive of the Foundation for Defense of Democracies, joins Ross Douthat on “Interesting Times” to make the case that Operation Epic Fury has degraded Iran’s military power enough to make victory achievable.

item.title: Why Are So Many People Obsessed With Lindy West’s Polyamory?
item.description: Is Lindy West a feminist? Is she happy? Everyone has an opinion.

item.title: The Epstein Class Had a Signature Weakness
item.description: Epstein flourished by helping elite men reconcile their private desires with their public appearances.

item.title: Is It 1914 in America?
item.description: The planners of the Iran war had plenty of on-the-ground intelligence but operated with little insight into the minds of their enemies.

item.title: How to Straighten Out the Hormuz Bottleneck
item.description: Nations are looking for alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump Says Birthright Citizenship Was Only for the Children of Slaves. He’s Wrong.
item.description: The framers of the 14th Amendment endorsed a capacious definition of ‘American.’

item.title: Support for Israel Is Collapsing. Netanyahu Sees That.
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel is racing to eliminate regional threats because he sees that U.S. support for the country is collapsing, Mona Charen, the policy editor at The Bulwark, argues on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Don’t Cheer Too Hard for the Facebook Verdicts
item.description: Courts aren’t always the right answer to our digital problems.

item.title: Trump Revels in the Deaths of His Political Enemies
item.description: The president’s depravity is deathless

item.title: No Kings: Vast Throngs Say No to Trump
item.description: Readers discuss the huge rallies on Saturday protesting the president.

item.title: Your Chatbot Isn’t a Therapist
item.description: Patients are turning to A.I. chatbots for therapy — and the chatbots are fueling their worst impulses.

item.title: Billionaires, Stop Whining About the Giving Pledge and Try Giving Yourselves
item.description: It makes no sense to me that billionaires would criticize others for trying to help their fellow humans.

item.title: I Saw Something New in San Francisco
item.description: Marshall McLuhan was right about Claude, too.

item.title: Iran Thinks It Can Win a Long War
item.description: A costly war will make Iran’s enemies think twice before attacking again.

item.title: Stephen Miller’s Latest Low
item.description: His project of subordination at home and abroad continues unabated.

item.title: General McChrystal on the Myths Misleading Trump in Iran
item.description: The president may have been seduced by the idea of an easy victory, warns the former Iraq and Afghanistan war commander on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Democrats Need a New Promise: A House by 30
item.description: The federal government can once again make building wealth for young families a core mission.

item.title: Across Asia, Trump’s War on Iran Is Dangerous for Everyone
item.description: Trump’s war on Iran is dangerous for everyone.

item.title: How Far Will Trump Go in Iran?
item.description: I asked an Iran hawk what victory looks like now.

item.title: What Jackie and Ethel Kennedy Knew
item.description: “Love Story” wasn’t really about John Kennedy or Carolyn Bessette.

item.title: The Death Penalty’s Invisible Wounds
item.description: Readers respond to an editorial about the death penalty. Also: The benefits of small talk; calling all teenagers.

item.title: Goodbye, ‘Queer Eye.’ Goodbye, Queer Acceptance.
item.description: Today even tolerance is in doubt.

item.title: When Sports Heroes Have Terrible Opinions
item.description: Sports used to be an oasis from political squabbling. But now the bad opinions of star athletes have become impossible to escape.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz Closed Because Iran Is Using the U.S. Playbook
item.description: Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz by raising risks for ship operators. In doing so, it has taken lessons from American financial policy.

item.title: The Old Man Dreaming Up Wars for Young Men to Fight
item.description: Any deal with Iran would probably be a bad one. But sending ground troops to seize Iranian territory would be an even worse choice.

item.title: How We Stopped a Bid to Seat Election Deniers in Fulton County
item.description: Our local dispute should serve as a warning to all Americans about the midterms.

item.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Is Fun. Maybe That’s All It Takes.
item.description: There’s been a vibe shift in Hollywood. Fun movies are back.

item.title: ‘We Are Going to Deeply Regret This War’
item.description: Three journalists on the potential repercussions of Operation Epic Fury.

item.title: Trump Does Anything He Wants — and More
item.description: When you’re a wannabe king they let you do it.

item.title: Is Religion Reviving or Declining? Both.
item.description: On the debate over the future of religious faith, and if it is expanding or not, in America.

item.title: What We Have Learned From the Minnesota Resistance
item.description: Readers respond to Thomas L. Friedman’s column about the community spirit helping Minnesotans in their opposition to ICE.

item.title: ‘We Are Going to Deeply Regret This War’
item.description: Three journalists on the potential repercussions of Operation Epic Fury.

item.title: Mamdani’s Long Hours Are What Millennial Leadership Looks Like
item.description: There’s a real advantage to being young and governing: being able to work long hours. But there are potential tradeoffs to Mamdani's all-in management style.

item.title: Technology Weakens Our Minds. It’s Time to Resist.
item.description: Just as we changed our thinking around physical fitness, we need to change our attitude toward cognitive fitness.

item.title: Trump on the Dollar: Dismay and Derision
item.description: Reaction to President Trump’s signature on the dollar. Also: Innocent lives lost in Iran; Democrats’ brand; young job seekers; subway announcements; calling all teenagers.

item.title: Republicans Know This War Is Going Badly
item.description: Never before has America arrived at the threshold of a quagmire so quickly.

item.title: The Terrible Cost of the Infinite Scroll
item.description: We are beginning an era in which the social media companies are finally held accountable for their harm.

item.title: It’s Elon Musk’s World Now
item.description: Is Muskism the new Fordism?

item.title: The War in Ukraine Changed the World in Ways We’re Only Starting to Comprehend
item.description: Open-ended military conflict in Europe could, at some point, intersect with open-ended military conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: How Far Will Trump Go in Iran?
item.description: I asked an Iran hawk what victory looks like now.

item.title: Will Iran Break Trumpism?
item.description: The right-wing thinker Christopher Caldwell believes the Iran war is the end of Trumpism. Is he right? What was Trumpism in the first place?

item.title: Trump Said This Policy Would Make Manhattan a ‘Ghost Town.’ He Was Wrong.
item.description: The success of congestion pricing is a reminder that government, done right, has immense power to improve people’s lives.

item.title: Men of the Trump Administration, 2026
item.description: Are Democrats ugly? Asking for a friend.

item.title: Contemporary Opera Doesn’t Need to ‘Challenge’ Audiences
item.description: With a musical language they can appreciate, Americans could more easily find opera’s appeal.

item.title: Memes and Mixed Messages on the Iran War
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by Phil Klay. Also: Educating undocumented students; energy realities; calling all teenagers.

item.title: How Can America Be So Miserable When It’s So Rich?
item.description: The just resentments of a shrinking middle class.
